## Overview
Complete the pending homework assignment for Logic for Computer Scientists. This assignment involves solving logic problems, constructing proofs, and demonstrating understanding of course concepts.

## Course Information
- **Course**: Logic for Computer Scientists
- **Semester**: Fall 2025 (or as applicable)
- **Assignment**: Homework 3 (or current assignment)
- **Status**: In progress / Pending completion

## Assignment Details

### Existing Files
- `_assignments/logic-for-computer-scientists/logic-homework3.md` - Assignment problems and instructions
- `_assignments/logic-for-computer-scientists/logic-homework3-solutions.md` - Solutions document (may be partial)
- `_posts/2026-01-13-homework-3-guide.md` - Study guide/reference post

### Assignment Topics
Based on existing files, this assignment covers:
- Herbrand semantics
- Propositional logic
- CNF (Conjunctive Normal Form)
- Proof construction
- Logical reasoning

## Requirements

### Problem Solving
- [ ] Read and understand all assignment problems
- [ ] Solve each problem step-by-step
- [ ] Show all work and reasoning
- [ ] Verify solutions for correctness
- [ ] Check solutions against course materials and textbook

### Documentation
- [ ] Solutions clearly written and formatted
- [ ] Mathematical notation properly formatted (KaTeX)
- [ ] Proofs are complete and rigorous
- [ ] Explanations provided for each step
- [ ] Final answers clearly marked

### Technical Requirements
- [ ] Solutions document updated in `_assignments/logic-for-computer-scientists/logic-homework3-solutions.md`
- [ ] Markdown formatting follows project template
- [ ] All LaTeX/math notation renders correctly
- [ ] Code examples (if any) are properly formatted
- [ ] Diagrams or visual aids included if helpful

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] All problems attempted and solved
- [ ] Solutions are correct and complete
- [ ] Work is neat and organized
- [ ] Notation is consistent throughout
- [ ] Proofs follow proper logical structure
- [ ] References to course materials included
- [ ] Solutions match assignment requirements

## Deliverables

1. **Completed Solutions**: All problems solved with full work shown
2. **Updated Solutions Document**: `_assignments/logic-for-computer-scientists/logic-homework3-solutions.md` fully completed
3. **Verification**: Solutions checked for correctness
4. **Documentation**: Solutions properly formatted and documented
5. **Submission**: Assignment ready for submission (if applicable)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All assignment problems completed
- [ ] Solutions are correct and verified
- [ ] All work shown with clear explanations
- [ ] Mathematical notation properly formatted
- [ ] Solutions document is complete and well-organized
- [ ] Assignment meets all stated requirements
- [ ] Ready for submission or review

## Notes

- Review course materials and lecture notes before starting
- Work through problems systematically
- Verify solutions using multiple methods if possible
- Seek help from instructor or TAs if stuck on specific problems
- Double-check all mathematical notation and formatting
- Ensure solutions follow the logical structure taught in class
- Consider creating a study guide or summary if helpful
